threat_model: Browser observation can accidentally become browser control or private-data capture if URL, DOM, screenshot, cookie, session, secret, click, submit, keystroke, navigation, connector, or publication boundaries are not explicit. The mitigation is a digest-only receipt schema and validator that reject raw storage and deny all mutation authority in Foundation Mode.

required_checks.check: browser navigation, click, submit, keystroke, connector, publication, and terminal authority remain false
redaction check confirms URL, DOM, screenshot, and title evidence remain digest refs
IDOR check confirms raw URL, DOM, screenshot, and secret storage remain denied and tenant scope stays foundation-local-only
receipt integrity check confirms capture policy, evidence classification, UAO, and LifeMeaningJudgment refs are retained
audit visibility check confirms schema and protocol manifest validation retain browser observation receipt evidence
proof coverage matrix includes the browser observation receipt surface

findings.mitigation: Foundation Mode receipt blocks browser navigation, click, submit, keystroke injection, cookie/session reads, connector calls, external writes, publication, terminal closure, and success claims.
Foundation Mode receipt requires hash-only URL evidence, digest refs, raw storage denial, private payload redaction, and operator review.

mitigations: keep BrowserObservationReceipt validation non-live and deterministic
require digest refs instead of raw URL, DOM, screenshot, title, cookie, session, or secret payloads
require operator-local consent and UAO refs
deny browser navigation, click, submit, keystroke injection, connector calls, and external writes
deny publication, terminal closure, and success claims in Foundation Mode
